title: 1.1: Tests that sane() returns dots for non-printable characters when given bytes with ASCII codes < 32 or >= 127
1.2: Tests that lhex() returns appropriate hex string representation when given integers, tuples, and lists
1.3: Tests that restart() executes without error when mocked and conf.interactive is True
1.4: Tests that linehexdump() returns formatted hex and ASCII representation on a single line
1.5: Tests that hexstr() returns hex and sanitized ASCII representation when given mixed printable/non-printable bytes
2.1: Places the test for string sanitization with non-printable characters in test/regression.uts
2.2: Places the test for hex representation conversion in test/regression.uts
2.3: Places the test for Scapy restart functionality in test/regression.uts
2.4: Places the test for line-based hexdump output in test/regression.uts
2.5: Places the test for hex string conversion in test/regression.uts
3.1: All tests use the UTscapy framework with '= Test name' section headers
3.2: All tests follow the '= Test <function_name> function' naming pattern
3.3: All tests use bare boolean expressions or Python assert statements for validation
4.1: Follows the pattern of using mock decorators for test case testing restart functionality
